ddls:
CREATE TABLE "parameters" (
  "parameter_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each parameter */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the parameter */,
  "access" TEXT /* Access level (Read/Write or Read only) */,
  "prompt" TEXT /* Description of the parameter's functionality */,
  "data_type" TEXT /* Data type of the parameter value */,
  "comment" TEXT /* Additional comments regarding the parameter */,
  "min_value" TEXT /* Minimum allowed value for the parameter */,
  "max_value" TEXT /* Maximum allowed value for the parameter */,
  "default_value" TEXT /* Default value of the parameter */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the parameter */,
  "tolerance" TEXT /* Tolerance level for the parameter value */,
  "is_archivable" INTEGER /* Indicates if the parameter value should be archived */,
  "is_mandatory" INTEGER /* Indicates if the parameter is mandatory */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("parameter_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for system login */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., operator, engineer, admin) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user (hashed) */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login date of the user */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user account is active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date when the user account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "parameter_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parameter changed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the change */,
  "change_date" TEXT /* Date when the change was made */,
  "old_value" TEXT /* Previous value of the parameter */,
  "new_value" TEXT /* New value of the parameter */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user who made the change */,
  "session_id" TEXT /* Session ID of the user who made the change */,
  "is_automated" INTEGER /* Indicates if the change was made by an automated process */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_logs_parameter_id FOREIGN KEY ("parameter_id") REFERENCES parameters ("parameter_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "devices" (
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each device */,
  "device_name" TEXT /* Name of the device */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of device (e.g., sensor, actuator) */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current operational status of the device (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  "serial_number" TEXT /* Serial number of the device */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date when the device was installed */,
  "last_maintenance" TEXT /* Date of the last maintenance */,
  "next_maintenance" TEXT /* Date of the next scheduled maintenance */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the device in the production line */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the device */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model of the device */,
  "is_connected" INTEGER /* Indicates if the device is currently connected to the system */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "device_parameters" (
  "device_param_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each device-parameter mapping */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device */,
  "parameter_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parameter associated with the device */,
  "min_value" TEXT /* Minimum allowed value for the parameter on this device */,
  "max_value" TEXT /* Maximum allowed value for the parameter on this device */,
  "default_value" TEXT /* Default value of the parameter on this device */,
  "is_override" INTEGER /* Indicates if the parameter value can be overridden for this device */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("device_param_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_device_parameters_parameter_id FOREIGN KEY ("parameter_id") REFERENCES parameters ("parameter_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_device_parameters_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "parameter_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parameter that triggered the alert */,
  "threshold_value" TEXT /* Threshold value that triggered the alert */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* Message describing the alert */,
  "alert_date" TEXT /* Date when the alert was generated */,
  "is_resolved" INTEGER /* Indicates if the alert has been resolved */,
  "resolved_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who resolved the alert */,
  "resolved_date" TEXT /* Date when the alert was resolved */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the alert (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_resolved_by FOREIGN KEY ("resolved_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_parameter_id FOREIGN KEY ("parameter_id") REFERENCES parameters ("parameter_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "settings" (
  "setting_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each setting */,
  "setting_name" TEXT /* Name of the global setting */,
  "value" TEXT /* Value of the setting */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the setting */,
  "is_overrideable" INTEGER /* Indicates if the setting can be overridden by users */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date when the setting was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date when the setting was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("setting_id")
)
